perturbation.original_text: 3. Receiving Party acknowledges that Confidential Information is valuable, proprietary and confidential. Unless other wise expressly authorized by Disclosing Party, Receiving Party agrees to retain the Confidential Information in confidence for a period as stipulated in Clause 7 herein, and shall not disclose the Confidential Information to any third party or any employee or agent (except its officers, employees, representatives, advisors or agents who have a need to know) and shall not use the Confidential Information for any purpose other than for the purposes agree to herein. No license expressed or implied in the Confi dential Information is granted to either party other than to use the information in the minor and to the extent authorised by this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. Receiving Party acknowledges that Confidential Information is valuable, proprietary and confidential. Unless other wise expressly authorized by Disclosing Party, Receiving Party agrees to retain the Confidential Data in confidence for a period as stipulated in Clause 7 herein, and shall not disclose the Confidential Information to any third party or any employee or agent (except its officers, employees, representatives, advisors or agents who have a need to know) and shall not use the Confidential Information for any purpose other than for the purposes agree to herein. No license expressed or implied in the Confi dential Information is granted to either party other than to use the information in the minor and to the extent authorised by this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Confidential Data' within the same paragraph that emphasizes the confidential nature of the information creates ambiguity. The document defines 'Confidential Information'. This in-text change suggests a possible distinction between 'Confidential Information' and 'Confidential Data' without definition, leading to disputes over which data is protected. This perturbs the original intention where confidential information is referred.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Receiving Party acknowledges that Confidential Information is valuable, proprietary and confidential. Unless other wise expressly authorized by Disclosing Party, Receiving Party agrees to retain the Confidential Data in confidence for a period as stipulated in Clause 7 herein, and shall not disclose the Confidential Information to any third party or any employee or agent (except its officers, employees, representatives, advisors or agents who have a need to know) and shall not use the Confidential Information for any purpose other than for the purposes agree to herein. No license expressed or implied in the Confi dential Information is granted to either party other than to use the information in the minor and to the extent authorised by this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Confidential Information' to 'Confidential Data' introduces a misaligned terminology contradiction. The document defines 'Confidential Information' but not 'Confidential Data'. This creates ambiguity as to whether 'Confidential Data' falls under the same protections and obligations as 'Confidential Information,' contradicting the paragraph's original intention to uniformly protect 'Confidential Information'. The contradicted text, which is the same as the changed text, shows this change being implemented causing the contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 6. All Confidential Information is provided “as it is”. In no event shall Disclosing Party be liable for the inaccuracy or incompleteness of the Confid ential Information. None of the Confidential Information disclosed by the parties constitutes any representation, warranty, assurance, guarantee or inducement by either party to the other with respect to the fitness of such Confidential Information for any particular purpose or infringement of trademarks, patents, copyrights or any right of third parties.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. All Confidential Information is provided “as it is”. In no event shall Disclosing Party be liable for the inaccuracy or incompleteness of the Protected Information. None of the Confidential Information disclosed by the parties constitutes any representation, warranty, assurance, guarantee or inducement by either party to the other with respect to the fitness of such Confidential Information for any particular purpose or infringement of trademarks, patents, copyrights or any right of third parties.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Protected Information' in the second sentence of Clause 6. The clause disclaims liability for inaccuracy of 'Protected Information', but later refers to 'Confidential Information'. The undefined 'Protected Information' introduces ambiguity, allowing the Disclosing Party to argue that disclaimed liability only applies to 'Protected Information' and not the defined 'Confidential Information', even though the overall intention was to disclaim for all disclosed information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All Confidential Information is provided “as it is”. In no event shall Disclosing Party be liable for the inaccuracy or incompleteness of the Protected Information. None of the Confidential Information disclosed by the parties constitutes any representation, warranty, assurance, guarantee or inducement by either party to the other with respect to the fitness of such Confidential Information for any particular purpose or infringement of trademarks, patents, copyrights or any right of third parties.
perturbation.justification: The change replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Protected Information' in the second sentence of Clause 6. The original text and contradicted text contain the changed sentence. This creates a contradiction because the agreement defines 'Confidential Information', but 'Protected Information' is undefined, leading to ambiguity about which information the Disclosing Party is liable for. This misalignment of terminology creates an in-text contradiction, as the contract uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations as described in the Perturbation Explanation and the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: 7. This Agreement shall be effective from the date hereof and shall terminate upon the completion of any transaction in relation to the underlying transacti on or otherwise as determined by the parties; however, the obligations of Receiving Party respecting disclosure and confidentiality shall continue to be binding and applicable without limit in time except and, until such information enters the public domai n save and except the clause (2) herein.
perturbation.changed_text: 7. This Agreement shall be effective from the date hereof and shall terminate upon the completion of any transaction in relation to the underlying transacti on or otherwise as determined by the parties; however, the obligations of the Recipient respecting disclosure and confidentiality shall continue to be binding and applicable without limit in time except and, until such information enters the public domai n save and except the clause (2) herein.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Receiving Party' to 'Recipient' in Clause 7 introduces ambiguity regarding who is bound by confidentiality after the agreement terminates. 'Receiving Party' is defined earlier. The inconsistency can lead to disputes over whether the termination clause applies to both 'Receiving Party' and 'Recipient' or just one of them. Within the same clause, both terms are being used, increasing the contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall be effective from the date hereof and shall terminate upon the completion of any transaction in relation to the underlying transacti on or otherwise as determined by the parties; however, the obligations of the Recipient respecting disclosure and confidentiality shall continue to be binding and applicable without limit in time except and, until such information enters the public domai n save and except the clause (2) herein.
perturbation.justification: The change replaces 'Receiving Party' with 'Recipient' in Clause 7. The explanation states that 'Receiving Party' is defined earlier in the document, whereas 'Recipient' is not. This introduces ambiguity and a potential contradiction because the contradicted text also uses 'Recipient' in the exact same clause. The change directly leads to a conflict over who is bound by the confidentiality obligations after termination.
